pr.title: fix(postinstall+hooks): bundle jq binary alongside hooks (closes #165, supersedes #174)
pr.summary: Postinstall now downloads a pinned jq 1.7.1 binary from the official jqlang/jq GitHub release and lands it at ~/.claude/hooks/bin/jq. Every hook resolves jq via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]}/bin/jq` first, with `command -v jq` as a fallback so the in-repo test suite still works before postinstall has run. Mirrors the @playwright/cli + chromium delivery idiom (postinstall fetches an external binary on the consumer's behalf, no extra npm deps). Closes #165 (hooks crash when jq is not on PATH); supersedes #174 (which only detected + warned).

design.argumentOrderConventionReason: PR is a postinstall + harness-hook change. No element-interactions src/ public API added; the (elementName, pageName, ...) convention does not apply.
design.asyncEverywhereReason: Bash-script hook + Node postinstall edits — no JavaScript/TypeScript public API surface. The new postinstall does use async for the https download + atomic-rename, but that's an internal install-time mechanic, not a runtime API.
design.stepsKeptLightweightReason: No additions to Steps; PR is install-time mechanic + hook preamble change only.
design.namingConventionReason: No new public methods named. The new postinstall function follows the existing camelCase convention (installBundledJq, installChromium) sibling to installCivitasHooks.
design.presenceDetectInActionsReason: No new action methods; PR is postinstall + hooks only.
design.loggingPresentReason: Postinstall console.log / console.warn follows the existing [civitas-cerebrum] prefix pattern. Hook FATAL message uses the existing project-standard format. The runtime tester:* logging categories are scoped to the Steps API, not to harness mechanics.
design.typescriptDisciplineReason: Hooks are bash, postinstall is plain Node JS — no TypeScript src/ touched.

tests.exerciseRealVueAppReason: Hook tests are pipe-fixture tests; the Vue test app applies to runtime Steps API tests, which this PR does not modify. Postinstall was manually verified end-to-end in a sandbox (download → chmod → version probe → idempotent re-run → CIVITAS_SKIP_JQ_INSTALL opt-out).
specFiles: hooks/tests/run.sh — full suite re-exercises every modified hook through its existing case files (cases/*.sh); preamble + JQ resolution covered transitively. No new case files needed: the preamble is shared infrastructure, not a new rule.

build.knownFailures: Hook test suite green at 435/435 (`bash hooks/tests/run.sh`). Manually verified: postinstall in /tmp sandbox downloads jq-1.7.1 (807KB) to ~/.claude/hooks/bin/jq, --version reports jq-1.7.1, idempotent skip on re-run, CIVITAS_SKIP_JQ_INSTALL=1 opt-out clean. Tests still green when bundled jq is moved aside (system-jq fallback path).

coverage.apiCoverageGate100Reason: PR adds no public API on Steps / ElementAction / matcher tree — the `npx test-coverage` 100% gate is a no-op for this PR's diff.

docs.readmeUpdatedReason: README does not currently mention jq, and the user spec for this PR explicitly carved README out of scope. Postinstall behaviour change is install-time only and visible via the existing [civitas-cerebrum] log line.
docs.apiReferenceUpdatedReason: No public Steps / ElementAction / matcher API touched.
docs.skillFilesUpdatedReason: Sweep of skills/ for stale 'jq must be on PATH' or 'install jq manually' prose found none — the only `\bjq\b` matches are inside the contributing-skill's hook-authoring template (showing emit_deny / emit_warn helper shapes), which are correct as-is. Per the PR #187 doctrine ('skills cite hooks by existence + purpose + path; don't re-explain mechanics'), no new skill prose was added either.

version.patchBumpedOnceReason: Per the user-directive 'no version bumps unless explicitly authorised' (2026-05-06): this PR leaves package.json + package-lock.json at main's 0.3.6.
